revision.text:
'''カトンボ山古墳'''（かとんぼやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]] [[堺市]]赤畑町にかつてあった[[古墳時代]]中期の[[古墳]]（[[円墳]]）である。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]の中の１基であった。
== 概要== [[陵墓参考地]]の[[御廟山古墳]]の後円部の濠外にあり、昭和２４年（１９４９年）に土取り工事によって消滅にしている。直径５０ｍの円墳と推定されている。土取りによる破壊が突如行なわれたが、緊急調査が古代学研究会の[[森浩一]]らによって行なわれている。出土した遺物は通常、古墳副葬品とよんでいるものとはかなり違っており、[[滑石]]製の祭祀遺物を大量に含んでいた。滑石製品は、子持勾玉４個、[[勾玉]]７２５個、斧頭６個、鎌１３個、刀子１６０個、双孔円板１個、鍬形（剣身形）１個、臼玉約２万個があった。このうち斧頭、鎌、刀子は本来は鉄製利器であるものを[[祭祀]]用として滑石で製作しており、実用には使えない石製模造品である。また、勾玉も装身具ではなく滑石で簡便に製作したものであり、臼玉もガラス製小玉などの代用品であろうとしている。玉類に孔が貫通しているのは当然として、斧頭、鎌、刀子にも必ず１孔か２孔あけられており、これらの使用方法としては、玉類を延々と連ねて、おそらく樹木の枝にかけ、斧頭、鎌、刀子もそれぞれ個々に懸垂させ、クリスマス・ツリーのように飾ったのものではないかとしている。滑石製品のほかには２面の[[銅鏡]]と刀、剣、矛、鏃、刀子、斧頭、蜘蛛手形鉄器などの鉄製品があったが、百舌鳥古墳群や[[古市古墳群]]の古墳によく見られる甲冑類がなく、大量の滑石製品が大量に埋納され、[[朱]]を使用した形跡もなく、しかも人体埋葬の行なわれたことを示す資料もなかったようである。大型古墳に附属した位置にある陪塚に大量に遺物を埋納しながら、人体埋葬の形跡のない類例の１つとしている。
、
==参考文献 == [[森浩一]] 『大阪府史 第一巻』 大阪府史編纂委員会 １９７８年 ６９２項～６９４項
[[category:古墳]]
[[category:円墳]]
[[category:大阪府にある国の史跡]]
[[category:大阪府の古墳]]
[[category:堺市]]

'''カトンボ山古墳'''（かとんぼやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]] [[堺市]]赤畑町にかつてあった[[古墳時代]]中期の[[古墳]]（[[円墳]]）である。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]の中の１基であった。
== 概要== [[陵墓参考地]]の[[御廟山古墳]]の後円部の濠外にあり、昭和２４年（１９４９年）に土取り工事によって消滅にしている。直径５０ｍの円墳と推定されている。土取りによる破壊が突如行なわれたが、緊急調査が古代学研究会の[[森浩一]]らによって行なわれている。出土した遺物は通常、古墳副葬品とよんでいるものとはかなり違っており、[[滑石]]製の祭祀遺物を大量に含んでいた。滑石製品は、子持勾玉４個、[[勾玉]]７２５個、斧頭６個、鎌１３個、刀子１６０個、双孔円板１個、鍬形（剣身形）１個、臼玉約２万個があった。このうち斧頭、鎌、刀子は本来は鉄製利器であるものを[[祭祀]]用として滑石で製作しており、実用には使えない石製模造品である。また、勾玉も装身具ではなく滑石で簡便に製作したものであり、臼玉もガラス製小玉などの代用品であろうとしている。玉類に孔が貫通しているのは当然として、斧頭、鎌、刀子にも必ず１孔か２孔あけられており、これらの使用方法としては、玉類を延々と連ねて、おそらく樹木の枝にかけ、斧頭、鎌、刀子もそれぞれ個々に懸垂させ、クリスマス・ツリーのように飾ったのものではないかとしている。滑石製品のほかには２面の[[銅鏡]]と刀、剣、矛、鏃、刀子、斧頭、蜘蛛手形鉄器などの鉄製品があったが、百舌鳥古墳群や[[古市古墳群]]の古墳によく見られる甲冑類がなく、大量の滑石製品が大量に埋納され、[[朱]]を使用した形跡もなく、しかも人体埋葬の行なわれたことを示す資料もなかったようである。大型古墳に附属した位置にある陪塚に大量に遺物を埋納しながら、人体埋葬の形跡のない類例の１つとしている。
、
==参考文献 == [[森浩一]] 『大阪府史 第一巻』 大阪府史編纂委員会 １９７８年 ６９２項～６９４項
[[category:古墳]]
[[category:円墳]]
[[category:大阪府の古墳]]
[[category:堺市]]
[[category:堺市の歴史]]

== 概要== [[陵墓参考地]]の[[御廟山古墳]]の後円部の濠外にあり、昭和24年（1949年）に土取り工事によって消滅している。直径50ｍの円墳と推定されている。土取りによる破壊が突如行なわれたが、緊急調査が古代学研究会の[[森浩一]]らによって行なわれている。出土した遺物は通常、古墳副葬品とよんでいるものとはかなり違っており、[[滑石]]製の祭祀遺物を大量に含んでいた。滑石製品は、子持勾玉4個、[[勾玉]]725個、斧頭6個、鎌13個、刀子160個、双孔円板1個、鍬形（剣身形）1個、臼玉約2万個があった。このうち斧頭、鎌、刀子は本来は鉄製利器であるものを[[祭祀]]用として滑石で製作しており、実用には使えない石製模造品である。また、勾玉も装身具ではなく滑石で簡便に製作したものであり、臼玉もガラス製小玉などの代用品であろうとしている。玉類に孔が貫通しているのは当然として、斧頭、鎌、刀子にも必ず1孔か2孔あけられており、これらの使用方法としては、玉類を延々と連ねて、おそらく樹木の枝にかけ、斧頭、鎌、刀子もそれぞれ個々に懸垂させ、クリスマス・ツリーのように飾ったのものではないかとしている。滑石製品のほかには２面の[[銅鏡]]と刀、剣、矛、鏃、刀子、斧頭、蜘蛛手形鉄器などの鉄製品があったが、百舌鳥古墳群や[[古市古墳群]]の古墳によく見られる甲冑類がなく、大量の滑石製品が大量に埋納され、[[朱]]を使用した形跡もなく、しかも人体埋葬の行なわれたことを示す資料もなかったようである。大型古墳に附属した位置にある陪塚に大量に遺物を埋納しながら、人体埋葬の形跡のない類例の1つとしている。

{{日本の古墳 |名称= カトンボ山古墳 |画像= |所在地= 大阪府堺市北区百舌鳥赤畑町 |位置= |形状= 円墳 |規模=径50ｍ |築造年代= 5世紀 |埋葬施設=粘土槨 |被葬者= |出土品=滑石製玉類 銅鏡 鉄刀 |史跡指定=国指定史跡 |特記事項= }}
'''カトンボ山古墳'''（かとんぼやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]] [[堺市]]北区赤畑町にかつてあった[[古墳時代]]中期の[[古墳]]（[[円墳]]）である。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]の中の1基であった。
== 概要== [[陵墓参考地]]の[[御廟山古墳]]の後円部の濠外にあり、昭和24年（1949年）に土取り工事によって消滅している。直径50ｍの円墳と推定されている。土取りによる破壊が突如行なわれたが、緊急調査が古代学研究会の[[森浩一]]らによって行なわれている。出土した遺物は通常、古墳副葬品とよんでいるものとはかなり違っており、[[滑石]]製の祭祀遺物を大量に含んでいた。滑石製品は、子持勾玉4個、[[勾玉]]725個、斧頭6個、鎌13個、刀子160個、双孔円板1個、鍬形（剣身形）1個、臼玉約2万個があった。このうち斧頭、鎌、刀子は本来は鉄製利器であるものを[[祭祀]]用として滑石で製作しており、実用には使えない石製模造品である。また、勾玉も装身具ではなく滑石で簡便に製作したものであり、臼玉もガラス製小玉などの代用品であろうとしている。玉類に孔が貫通しているのは当然として、斧頭、鎌、刀子にも必ず1孔か2孔あけられており、これらの使用方法としては、玉類を延々と連ねて、おそらく樹木の枝にかけ、斧頭、鎌、刀子もそれぞれ個々に懸垂させ、クリスマス・ツリーのように飾る[[神事]]に使われたものではないかとしている。滑石 製品のほかには２面の[[銅鏡]]と刀、剣、矛、鏃、刀子、斧頭、蜘蛛手形鉄器などの鉄製品があったが、百舌鳥古墳群や[[古市古墳群]]の古墳によく見られる甲冑類がなく、大量の滑石製品が大量に埋納され、[[朱]]を使用した形跡もなく、しかも人体埋葬の行なわれたことを示す資料もなかったようである。大型古墳に附属した位置にある陪塚に大量に遺物を埋納しながら、人体埋葬の形跡のない類例の1つではないかとされている。
==参考文献 == [[森浩一]]『大阪府史 第一巻』大阪府史編纂委員会 1978年 692項～694項
== 関連項目== *[[百舌鳥古墳群]]

'''カトンボ山古墳'''（かとんぼやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]] [[堺市]]北区赤畑町にかつてあった[[古墳時代]]中期の[[古墳]]（[[円墳]]）である。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]の中の1基であった。
== 概要== [[陵墓参考地]]の[[御廟山古墳]]の後円部の濠外にあり、昭和24年（1949年）に土取り業者による採土工事によって消滅している。直径50メートルの円墳と推定されている。土取りによる破壊が突如行なわれたが、緊急調査が学生考古学研究会（現在の古代学研究会）の[[森浩一]]らによって行なわれている。出土した遺物は通常、古墳副葬品とよんでいるものとはかなり違っており、[[滑石]]製の祭祀遺物を大量に含んでいた。滑石製品は、子持勾玉4個、[[勾玉]]725個、斧頭6個、鎌13個、刀子160個、双孔円板1個、鍬形（剣身形）1個、臼玉約2万個があった。このうち斧頭、鎌、刀子は本来は鉄製利器であるものを[[祭祀]]用として滑石で製作しており、実用には使えない石製模造品である。また、勾玉も装身具ではなく滑石で簡便に製作したものであり、臼玉もガラス製小玉などの代用品であろうとしている。玉類に孔が貫通しているのは当然として、斧頭、鎌、刀子にも必ず1孔か2孔あけられており、これらの使用方法としては、玉類を延々と連ねて、おそらく樹木の枝にかけ、斧頭、鎌、刀子もそれぞれ個々に懸垂させ、クリスマス・ツリーのように飾る[[神事]]に使われたものではないかとしている。滑石製品のほかには２面の[[銅鏡]]（位至三公鏡と無文鏡）と刀、剣、矛、鏃、刀子、斧頭、蜘蛛手形鉄器などの鉄製品があったが、百舌鳥古墳群や[[古市古墳群]]の古墳によく見られる甲冑類がなく、大量の滑石製品が大量に埋納され、[[朱]]を使用した形跡もなく、しかも人体埋葬の行なわれたことを示す資料もなかったようである。大型古墳に附属した位置にある陪塚に大量に遺物を埋納しながら、人体埋葬の形跡のない類例の1つではないかとされている。
==参考文献 == [[森浩一]]『大阪府史 第一巻』大阪府史編纂委員会 1978年 692項～694項 森浩一 「カトンボ山古墳の発掘」『僕は考古学に鍛えられた』ちくま文庫 も26-1 筑摩書房 2012年 182-184頁 == 関連項目== *[[百舌鳥古墳群]]
== 外部リンク== [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html カトンボ山古墳 市デジタル古墳百科]
{{デフォルトソート:かとんほやまこふん}} [[category:円墳]] [[Category:百舌鳥古墳群|廃かとんほやまこふん]]

'''カトンボ山古墳'''（かとんぼやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]]北区赤畑町にかつてあった[[古墳時代]]中期の[[古墳]]（[[円墳]]）である。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]の中の1基であった。
== 概要== [[陵墓参考地]]の[[御廟山古墳]]の後円部の濠外にあり、昭和24年（1949年）に土取り業者による採土工事によって消滅している。直径50メートルの円墳と推定されている。土取りによる破壊が突如行われたが、緊急調査が学生考古学研究会（現在の古代学研究会）の[[森浩一]]らによって行われている。出土した遺物は通常、古墳副葬品とよんでいるものとはかなり異なり、[[滑石]]製の祭祀遺物を大量に含んでいた。滑石製品は、子持勾玉4個、[[勾玉]]725個、斧頭6個、鎌13個、刀子160個、双孔円板1個、鍬形（剣身形）1個、臼玉約2万個があった。このうち斧頭、鎌、刀子は本来は鉄製利器であるものを[[祭祀]]用として滑石で製作しており、実用には使えない石製模造品である。また、勾玉も装身具ではなく滑石で簡便に製作したものであり、臼玉もガラス製小玉などの代用品であろうとしている。玉類に孔が貫通しているのは自然として、斧頭、鎌、刀子にも必ず1孔か2孔あけられており、これらの使用方法としては、玉類を延々と連ねて、おそらく樹木の枝にかけ、斧頭、鎌、刀子もそれぞれ個々に懸垂させ、クリスマスツリーのように飾る[[神事]]に使われたものではないかとしている。滑石製品のほかには2面の[[銅鏡]]（位至三公鏡と無文鏡）と刀、剣、矛、鏃、刀子、斧頭、蜘蛛手形鉄器などの鉄製品があったが、百舌鳥古墳群や[[古市古墳群]]の古墳によく見られる甲冑類がなく、大量の滑石製品が大量に埋納され、[[朱]]を使用した形跡もなく、しかも人体埋葬の行なわれたことを示す資料もなかったようである。大型古墳に附属した位置にある陪塚に大量に遺物を埋納しながら、人体埋葬の形跡のない類例の1つではないかとされている。
== 参考文献 == * [[森浩一]]『大阪府史 第一巻』大阪府史編纂委員会 1978年 692項～694項 * 森浩一 「カトンボ山古墳の発掘」『僕は考古学に鍛えられた』ちくま文庫 も26-1 筑摩書房 2012年 182-184頁 == 関連項目== * [[百舌鳥古墳群]] * [[野中古墳]] - カトンボ山古墳と同様に多量の滑石製模造品・臼玉が埋納された陪塚。
== 外部リンク== * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html カトンボ山古墳 市デジタル古墳百科]
{{デフォルトソート:かとんほやまこふん}} [[category:円墳]] [[Category:百舌鳥古墳群|消かとんほやまこふん]]

'''カトンボ山古墳'''（かとんぼやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]]北区赤畑町にかつてあった[[古墳時代]]中期の[[古墳]]（[[円墳]]）である。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]の中の1基であった。
== 概要== [[ファイル:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|子持勾玉（凹面側）}}{{small|[[東京国立博物館]]展示。}}]] [[ファイル:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|子持勾玉（凸面側）}}{{small|[[鳥取県立博物館]]企画展示時に撮影。}}]] [[ファイル:カトンボ山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|出土品}}{{small|東京国立博物館蔵。[[松戸市立博物館]]企画展示時に撮影。}}]] [[陵墓参考地]]の[[御廟山古墳]]の後円部の濠外にあり、昭和24年（1949年）に土取り業者による採土工事によって消滅している。直径50メートルの円墳と推定されている。土取りによる破壊が突如行われたが、緊急調査が学生考古学研究会（現在の古代学研究会）の[[森浩一]]らによって行われている。出土した遺物は通常、古墳副葬品とよんでいるものとはかなり異なり、[[滑石]]製の祭祀遺物を大量に含んでいた。滑石製品は、子持勾玉4個、[[勾玉]]725個、斧頭6個、鎌13個、刀子160個、双孔円板1個、鍬形（剣身形）1個、臼玉約2万個があった。このうち斧頭、鎌、刀子は本来は鉄製利器であるものを[[祭祀]]用として滑石で製作しており、実用には使えない石製模造品である。また、勾玉も装身具ではなく滑石で簡便に製作したものであり、臼玉もガラス製小玉などの代用品であろうとしている。玉類に孔が貫通しているのは自然として、斧頭、鎌、刀子にも必ず1孔か2孔あけられており、これらの使用方法としては、玉類を延々と連ねて、おそらく樹木の枝にかけ、斧頭、鎌、刀子もそれぞれ個々に懸垂させ、クリスマスツリーのように飾る[[神事]]に使われたものではないかとしている。滑石製品のほかには2面の[[銅鏡]]（位至三公鏡と無文鏡）と刀、剣、矛、鏃、刀子、斧頭、蜘蛛手形鉄器などの鉄製品があったが、百舌鳥古墳群や[[古市古墳群]]の古墳によく見られる甲冑類がなく、滑石製品が大量に埋納され、[[朱]]を使用した形跡もなく、しかも人体埋葬の行なわれたことを示す資料もなかったようである。大型古墳に附属した位置にある陪塚に大量に遺物を埋納しながら、人体埋葬の形跡のない類例の1つではないかとされている。
== 参考文献 == * [[森浩一]]『大阪府史 第一巻』大阪府史編纂委員会 1978年 692-694頁 * 森浩一 「カトンボ山古墳の発掘」『僕は考古学に鍛えられた』ちくま文庫 も26-1 筑摩書房 2012年 182-184頁
== 関連項目== * [[百舌鳥古墳群]] * [[野中古墳]] - カトンボ山古墳と同様に多量の滑石製模造品・臼玉が埋納された陪塚。
== 外部リンク== * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html カトンボ山古墳 市デジタル古墳百科]
{{デフォルトソート:かとんほやまこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:円墳]] [[Category:百舌鳥古墳群|消かとんほやまこふん]]

revision.text:
{{Japanese Kofun Infobox |Name = Katonbo-yama Kofun |Image = |Group = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |Location = Mozu Akahata-cho, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |Coordinates = |Shape = Circular tumulus |Size = Diameter: 50 meters |Construction = 5th century |Burial Facility = Clay coffin |Occupant = |Artifacts = Steatite beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords |Historical Designation = |Notes = Destroyed }} '''Katonbo-yama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}) was a mid-[[Kofun period]] {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (tumulus) that once stood in {{ill|Mozu Akahata-cho|ja|百舌鳥赤畑町}}, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳}}, a reference mound designated as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. In 1949 (Showa 24), the mound was destroyed by soil extraction work carried out by contractors. The tumulus is estimated to have been a circular mound with a diameter of 50 meters. The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The artifacts unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual artifacts, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - Approximately 20,000 beads. These steatite artifacts included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other artifacts included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, pp. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, pp. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite artifacts and beads. == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html Katonbo-yama Kofun - Sakai City's Digital Kofun Encyclopedia] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katonboyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group|Katonboyama Kofun]]

{{Japanese Kofun Infobox |Name = Katonbo-yama Kofun |Image = |Group = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |Location = Mozu Akahata-cho, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |Coordinates = |Shape = Circular tumulus |Size = Diameter: 50 meters |Construction = 5th century |Burial Facility = Clay coffin |Occupant = |Artifacts = Steatite beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords |Historical Designation = |Notes = Destroyed }} '''Katonbo-yama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}) was a mid-[[Kofun period]] {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (tumulus) that once stood in {{ill|Mozu Akahata-cho|ja|百舌鳥赤畑町}}, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual artifacts, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - Approximately 20,000 beads. These steatite artifacts included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other artifacts included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, pp. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, pp. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite artifacts and beads. == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html Katonbo-yama Kofun - Sakai City's Digital Kofun Encyclopedia] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katonboyama Kofun}} [[Category:new pages]][[Categorry:Pages without Japanese interwiki]][[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:new pages]][[Categorry:Pages without Japanese interwiki]][[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[Category:new pages]][[Categorry:Pages without Japanese interwiki]][[Category:Mozu Kofun Group|Katonboyama Kofun]]

Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual artifacts, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - Approximately 20,000 beads. These steatite artifacts included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other artifacts included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, pp. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, pp. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳}} - The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other artifacts included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama

The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The artifacts unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual artifacts, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - Approximately 20,000 beads. These steatite artifacts included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other artifacts included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, pp. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, pp. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{Japanese Kofun Infobox |Name = Katonbo-yama Kofun |Image = |Group = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |Location = Mozu Akahata-cho, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |Coordinates = |Shape = Circular tumulus |Size = Diameter: 50 meters |Construction = 5th century |Burial Facility = Clay coffin |Occupant = |Artifacts = Steatite beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords |Historical Designation = |Notes = Destroyed }} '''Katonbo-yama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}) was a mid-[[Kofun period]] {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (tumulus) that once stood in {{ill|Mozu Akahata-cho|ja|百舌鳥赤畑町}}, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳}}, a reference mound designated as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. In 1949 (Showa 24), the mound was destroyed by soil extraction work carried out by contractors. The tumulus is estimated to have been a circular mound with a diameter of 50 meters. The artifacts unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. These steatite artifacts included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other artifacts included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, pp. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, pp. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite artifacts and beads. == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html Katonbo-yama Kofun - Sakai City's Digital Kofun Encyclopedia] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katonboyama Kofun}} [[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:I hope this works]][[ja:[[ja:[[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:I hope this works]][[ja:[[ja:[[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:I hope this works]][[ja:[[ja:[[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:I hope this works]][[ja:[[ja:[[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:I hope this works]][[ja:[[ja:[[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:I hope this works]][[ja:[[ja:[[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:Mozu Kofun Group|Katonboyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kk]][[ja:カトンボ山古墳]]

{{Japanese Kofun Infobox |Name = Katonbo-yama Kofun |Image = |Group = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |Location = Mozu Akahata-cho, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |Coordinates = |Shape = Circular tumulus |Size = Diameter: 50 meters |Construction = 5th century |Burial Facility = Clay coffin |Occupant = |Artifacts = Steatite beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords |Historical Designation = |Notes = Destroyed }} '''Katonbo-yama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}) was a mid-[[Kofun period]] {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (tumulus) that once stood in {{ill|Mozu Akahata-cho|ja|百舌鳥赤畑町}}, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳}}, a reference mound designated as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. In 1949 (Showa 24), the mound was destroyed by soil extraction work carried out by contractors. The tumulus is estimated to have been a circular mound with a diameter of 50 meters. The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The artifacts unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual artifacts, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - Approximately 20,000 beads. These steatite artifacts included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other artifacts included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama

The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other artifacts included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, pp. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, pp. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite artifacts and beads. == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html Katonbo-yama Kofun - Sakai City's Digital Kofun Encyclopedia] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katonboyama Kofun}} [[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:I hope this works]][[ja:[[ja:[[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:I hope this works]][[ja:[[ja:[[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:I hope this works]][[ja:[[ja:[[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:I hope this works]][[ja:[[ja:[[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:I hope this works]][[ja:[[ja:[[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:I hope this works]][[ja:[[ja:[[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:Mozu Kofun Group|Katonboyama Kofun]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:カトンボ山古墳]]

{{Japanese Kofun Infobox |Name = Katonbo-yama Kofun |Image = |Group = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |Location = Mozu Akahata-cho, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |Coordinates = |Shape = Circular tumulus |Size = Diameter: 50 meters |Construction = 5th century |Burial Facility = Clay coffin |Occupant = |Artifacts = Steatite beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords |Historical Designation = |Notes = Destroyed }} '''Katonbo-yama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}) was a mid-[[Kofun period]] {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (tumulus) that once stood in {{ill|Mozu Akahata-cho|ja|百舌鳥赤畑町}}, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳}}, a reference mound designated as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. In 1949 (Showa 24), the mound was destroyed by soil extraction work carried out by contractors. The tumulus is estimated to have been a circular mound with a diameter of 50 meters. The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The artifacts unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual artifacts, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - Approximately 20,000 beads. These steatite artifacts included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other artifacts included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, pp. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, pp. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite artifacts and beads. == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html Katonbo-yama Kofun - Sakai City's Digital Kofun Encyclopedia] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katonboyama Kofun}} [[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:I hope this works]][[ja:[[ja:[[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:I hope this works]][[ja:[[ja:[[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:I hope this works]][[ja:[[ja:[[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:I hope this works]][[ja:[[ja:[[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:I hope this works]][[ja:[[ja:[[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:I hope this works]][[ja:[[ja:[[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:Mozu Kofun Group|Katonboyama Kofun]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:カトンボ山古墳]]

It was part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳}}, a reference mound designated as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. In 1949 (Showa 24), the mound was destroyed by soil extraction work carried out by contractors. The tumulus is estimated to have been a circular mound with a diameter of 50 meters. The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The artifacts unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual artifacts, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - Approximately 20,000 beads. These steatite artifacts included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other artifacts included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, pp. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, pp. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite artifacts and beads. == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html Katonbo-yama Kofun - Sakai City's Digital Kofun Encyclopedia] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katonboyama Kofun}} [[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:I hope this works]][[ja:[[ja:[[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:I hope this works]][[ja:[[ja:[[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:I hope this works]][[ja:[[ja:[[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:I hope this works]][[ja:[[ja:[[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:I hope this works]][[ja:[[ja:[[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:I hope this works]][[ja:[[ja:[[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[ja:[[Category:Mozu Kofun Group|Katonboyama Kofun]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[ja:カトンボ山古墳]]

{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, pp. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, pp. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite artifacts and beads. == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html Katonbo-yama Kofun - Sakai City's Digital Kofun Encyclopedia] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katonboyama Kofun}} [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun [[Category:I hope this works]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[Category:I hope this works]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:I hope this works]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[Category:I hope this works]] [[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[Category:I hope this works]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[Category:I hope this works]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group|Katonboyama Kofun]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:カトンボ山古墳]]

revision.text:
{{Japanese Kofun Infobox |Name = Katonbo-yama Kofun |Image = |Group = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |Location = Mozu Akahata-cho, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |Coordinates = |Shape = Circular tumulus |Size = Diameter: 50 meters |Construction = 5th century |Burial Facility = Clay coffin |Occupant = |Artifacts = Steatite beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords |Historical Designation = |Notes = Destroyed }} '''Katonbo-yama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}) was a mid-[[Kofun period]] {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (tumulus) that once stood in {{ill|Mozu Akahata-cho|ja|百舌鳥赤畑町}}, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳}}, a reference mound designated as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. In 1949 (Showa 24), the mound was destroyed by soil extraction work carried out by contractors. The tumulus is estimated to have been a circular mound with a diameter of 50 meters. The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The artifacts unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual artifacts, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - Approximately 20,000 beads. These steatite artifacts included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other artifacts included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, pp. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, pp. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite artifacts and beads. == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html Katonbo-yama Kofun - Sakai City's Digital Kofun Encyclopedia] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katonboyama Kofun}} [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun [[Category:I hope this works]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[Category:I hope this works]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:I hope this works]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[Category:I hope this works]] [[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[Category:I hope this works]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[Category:I hope this works]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group|Katonboyama Kofun]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Katomboyama Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[category:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Katomboyama Kofun]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[ja:カトンボ山古墳]]

{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, pp. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, pp. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite artifacts and beads. == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html Katonbo-yama Kofun - Sakai City's Digital Kofun Encyclopedia] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katonboyama Kofun}} [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun [[Category:I hope this works]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[Category:I hope this works]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:I hope this works]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[Category:I hope this works]] [[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[Category:I hope this works]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[Category:I hope this works]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group|Katonboyama Kofun]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Katomboyama Kofun]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[category:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Katomboyama Kofun]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:カトンボ山古墳]]

revision.text:
{{Japanese Kofun Infobox |Name = Katonbo-yama Kofun |Image = |Group = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |Location = Mozu Akahata-cho, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |Coordinates = |Shape = Circular tumulus |Size = Diameter: 50 meters |Construction = 5th century |Burial Facility = Clay coffin |Occupant = |Artifacts = Steatite beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords |Historical Designation = |Notes = Destroyed }} '''Katonbo-yama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}) was a mid-[[Kofun period]] {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (tumulus) that once stood in {{ill|Mozu Akahata-cho|ja|百舌鳥赤畑町}}, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳}}, a reference mound designated as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. In 1949 (Showa 24), the mound was destroyed by soil extraction work carried out by contractors. The tumulus is estimated to have been a circular mound with a diameter of 50 meters. The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The artifacts unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual artifacts, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - Approximately 20,000 beads. These steatite artifacts included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other artifacts included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, pp. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, pp. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite artifacts and beads. == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html Katonbo-yama Kofun - Sakai City's Digital Kofun Encyclopedia] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katonboyama Kofun}} [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun [[Category:I hope this works]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[Category:I hope this works]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:I hope this works]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[Category:I hope this works]] [[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[Category:I hope this works]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[Category:I hope this works]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group|Katonboyama Kofun]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Katomboyama Kofun]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[category:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Pages needing attribution]][[Category:Katomboyama Kofun]][[Category:Pages needing attribution]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[ja:カトンボ山古墳]]

Other artifacts included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama

It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama

{{Japanese Kofun Infobox |Name = Katonbo-yama Kofun |Image = |Group = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |Location = Mozu Akahata-cho, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |Coordinates = |Shape = Circular tumulus |Size = Diameter: 50 meters |Construction = 5th century |Burial Facility = Clay coffin |Occupant = |Artifacts = Steatite beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords |Historical Designation = |Notes = Destroyed }} '''Katonbo-yama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}) was a mid-[[Kofun period]] {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (tumulus) that once stood in {{ill|Mozu Akahata-cho|ja|百舌鳥赤畑町}}, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳}}, a reference mound designated as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. In 1949 (Showa 24), the mound was destroyed by soil extraction work carried out by contractors. The tumulus is estimated to have been a circular mound with a diameter of 50 meters. The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The artifacts unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual artifacts, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - Approximately 20,000 beads. These steatite artifacts included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other artifacts included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, pp. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, pp. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite artifacts and beads. == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html Katonbo-yama Kofun - Sakai City's Digital Kofun Encyclopedia] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katonboyama Kofun}} [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun [[Category:I hope this works]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[Category:I hope this works]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:I hope this works]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[Category:I hope this works]] [[Category:Circular tumuli]] [[Category:I hope this works]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[Category:I hope this works]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group|Katonboyama Kofun]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Katomboyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Katomboyama Kofun]][[category:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Katomboyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Katomboyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]][[category:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[ja:カトンボ山古墳]]

It was part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳}}, a reference mound designated as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. In 1949 (Showa 24), the mound was destroyed by soil extraction work carried out by contractors. The tumulus is estimated to have been a circular mound with a diameter of 50 meters. The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The artifacts unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual artifacts, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - Approximately 20,000 beads. These steatite artifacts included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other artifacts included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, pp. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, pp. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{Japanese Kofun Infobox |Name = Katonbo-yama Kofun |Image = |Group = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |Location = Mozu Akahata-cho, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |Coordinates = |Shape = Circular tumulus |Size = Diameter: 50 meters |Construction = 5th century |Burial Facility = Clay coffin |Occupant = |Artifacts = Steatite beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords |Historical Designation = |Notes = Destroyed }} '''Katonbo-yama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}) was a mid-[[Kofun period]] {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (tumulus) that once stood in {{ill|Mozu Akahata-cho|ja|百舌鳥赤畑町}}, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳}}, a reference mound designated as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. In 1949 (Showa 24), the mound was destroyed by soil extraction work carried out by contractors. The tumulus is estimated to have been a circular mound with a diameter of 50 meters. The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The artifacts unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual artifacts, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - Approximately 20,000 beads. These steatite artifacts included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other artifacts included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, pp. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, pp. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite artifacts and beads. == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html Katonbo-yama Kofun - Sakai City's Digital Kofun Encyclopedia] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katonboyama Kofun}} [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun [[Category:I hope this works]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[Category:I hope this works]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:I hope this works]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[Category:I hope this works]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:I hope this works]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[Category:I hope this works]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group|Katonboyama Kofun]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Katomboyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Katomboyama Kofun]][[category:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Katomboyama Kofun-qq]] [[Category:Katomboyama Kofun]][[Category:pp]][[category:Katombo-yama Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:refreshing the redirects]] [[Category:making categorization show up on newly imported categories and templates]] [[Category:1 revision imported]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:refreshing so the massreplace can be done again]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Katomboyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katomboyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katomboyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katomboyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Katomboyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katomboyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katomboyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katomboyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Katomboyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katomboyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katomboyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katomboyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Katomboyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] [[Category:ppp]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{{Japanese Kofun Infobox |Name = Katonbo-yama Kofun |Image = |Group = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |Location = Mozu Akahata-cho, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |Coordinates = |Shape = Circular tumulus |Size = Diameter: 50 meters |Construction = 5th century |Burial Facility = Clay coffin |Occupant = |Artifacts = Steatite beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords |Historical Designation = |Notes = Destroyed }} '''Katonbo-yama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}) was a mid-[[Kofun period]] {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (tumulus) that once stood in {{ill|Mozu Akahata-cho|ja|百舌鳥赤畑町}}, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama

The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The artifacts unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual artifacts, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - Approximately 20,000 beads. These steatite artifacts included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other artifacts included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama

The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other artifacts included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Japanese Kofun Infobox" to "Infobox ancient site"
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |Name = Katonbo-yama Kofun |Image = |Group = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |Location = Mozu Akahata-cho, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |Coordinates = |Shape = Circular tumulus |Size = Diameter: 50 meters |Construction = 5th century |Burial Facility = Clay coffin |Occupant = |Artifacts = Steatite beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords |Historical Designation = |Notes = Destroyed }} '''Katonbo-yama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}) was a mid-[[Kofun period]] {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (tumulus) that once stood in {{ill|Mozu Akahata-cho|ja|百舌鳥赤畑町}}, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳}}, a reference mound designated as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. In 1949 (Showa 24), the mound was destroyed by soil extraction work carried out by contractors. The tumulus is estimated to have been a circular mound with a diameter of 50 meters. The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The artifacts unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual artifacts, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - Approximately 20,000 beads. These steatite artifacts included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other artifacts included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, pp. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, pp. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite artifacts and beads. == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html {{Infobox ancient site |Name = Katonbo-yama Kofun |Image = |Group = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} |Location = Mozu Akahata-cho, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |Coordinates = |Shape = Circular tumulus |Size = Diameter: 50 meters |Construction = 5th century |Burial Facility = Clay coffin |Occupant = |Artifacts = Steatite beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords |Historical Designation = |Notes = Destroyed }} '''Katonbo-yama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}) was a mid-[[Kofun period]] {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (tumulus) that once stood in {{ill|Mozu Akahata-cho|ja|百舌鳥赤畑町}}, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}}. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳}}, a reference mound designated as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. In 1949 (Showa 24), the mound was destroyed by soil extraction work carried out by contractors. The tumulus is estimated to have been a circular mound with a diameter of 50 meters. The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The artifacts unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual artifacts, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - Approximately 20,000 beads. These steatite artifacts included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other artifacts included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, pp. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, pp. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite artifacts and beads. == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |Name = Katonbo-yama Kofun |Image = |Group = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |Location = Mozu Akahata-cho, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |Coordinates = |Shape = Circular tumulus |Size = Diameter: 50 meters |Construction = 5th century |Burial Facility = Clay coffin |Occupant = |Artifacts = Steatite beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords |Historical Designation = |Notes = Destroyed }} '''Katonbo-yama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}) was a mid-[[Kofun period]] {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (tumulus) that once stood in {{ill|Mozu Akahata-cho|ja|百舌鳥赤畑町}}, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Excavated artifacts}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳}}, a reference mound designated as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地}}. In 1949 (Showa 24), the mound was destroyed by soil extraction work carried out by contractors. The tumulus is estimated to have been a circular mound with a diameter of 50 meters. The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The artifacts unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual artifacts, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - Aqqroximately 20,000 beads. These steatite artifacts included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other artifacts included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, qq. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, qq. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} * {{ill|Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite artifacts and beads. == External Links == * Other artifacts included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, qq. 692–694. * Koichi
