revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Katonbo-yama Kofun |image = | part_of = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |location = Mozu Akahata-cho, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = |type = Circular tumulus |area = Diameter: 50 meters |Construction = 5th century |burial = Clay coffin |Occupant = |burial goods = Steatite beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords |Historical Designation = |notes = Destroyed }} '''Katonbo-yama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}) was a mid-[[Kofun period]] {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (tumulus) that once stood in {{ill|Mozu Akahata-cho|ja|百舌鳥赤畑町}}, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|excavations}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳}}, a reference mound other_designation1 as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}. In 1949 (Showa 24), the mound was destroyed by soil extraction work carried out by contractors. The tumulus is estimated to have been a circular mound with a diameter of 50 meters. The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The burial goods unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual burial goods, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - A11roximately 20,000 beads. These steatite burial goods included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other burial goods included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, 11. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, 11. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} * {{ill|Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite burial goods and beads. == External Links == * The burial goods unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual burial goods, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - A11roximately 20,000 beads. These steatite burial goods included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other burial goods included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, 11. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, 11. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun It was part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|excavations}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳}}, a reference mound other_designation1 as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}. These steatite burial goods included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other burial goods included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Katonbo-yama Kofun |image = | part_of = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |location = Mozu Akahata-cho, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = |type = Circular tumulus |area = Diameter: 50 meters |Construction = 5th century |burial = Clay coffin |Occupant = |burial goods = Steatite beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords |Historical Designation = |notes = Destroyed }} '''Katonbo-yama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}) was a mid-[[Kofun period]] {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (tumulus) that once stood in {{ill|Mozu Akahata-cho|ja|百舌鳥赤畑町}}, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|excavations}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳}}, a reference mound other_designation1 as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}. In 1949 (Showa 24), the mound was destroyed by soil extraction work carried out by contractors. The tumulus is estimated to have been a circular mound with a diameter of 50 meters. The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The burial goods unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual burial goods, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - A11roximately 20,000 beads. These steatite burial goods included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other burial goods included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, 11. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, 11. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} * {{ill|Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite burial goods and beads. == External Links == * But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Katonbo-yama Kofun |image = | part_of = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |location = Mozu Akahata-cho, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = |type = Circular tumulus |area = Diameter: 50 meters |Construction = 5th century |burial = Clay coffin |Occupant = |burial goods = Steatite beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords |Historical designation1 = |notes = Destroyed }} '''Katonbo-yama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}) was a mid-[[Kofun period]] {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (tumulus) that once stood in {{ill|Mozu Akahata-cho|ja|百舌鳥赤畑町}}, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|excavations}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳}}, a reference mound other_designation1 as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}. In 1949 (Showa 24), the mound was destroyed by soil extraction work carried out by contractors. The tumulus is estimated to have been a circular mound with a diameter of 50 meters. The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The burial goods unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual burial goods, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - A11roximately 20,000 beads. These steatite burial goods included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other burial goods included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, 11. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, 11. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} * {{ill|Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite burial goods and beads. == External Links == * Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual burial goods, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - A11roximately 20,000 beads. These steatite burial goods included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other burial goods included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Katonbo-yama Kofun |image = | part_of = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |location = Mozu Akahata-cho, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = |type = Circular tumulus |area = Diameter: 50 meters |Construction = 5th century |burial = Clay coffin |Occupant = |burial goods = Steatite beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords |Historical designation1 = |notes = Destroyed }} '''Katonbo-yama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}) was a mid-[[Kofun period]] {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (tumulus) that once stood in {{ill|Mozu Akahata-cho|ja|百舌鳥赤畑町}}, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|excavations}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳}}, a reference mound other_designation1 as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}. In 1949 (Showa 24), the mound was destroyed by soil extraction work carried out by contractors. The tumulus is estimated to have been a circular mound with a diameter of 50 meters. The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The burial goods unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual burial goods, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - A11roximately 20,000 beads. These steatite burial goods included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other burial goods included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Katonbo-yama Kofun |image = | part_of = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |location = Mozu Akahata-cho, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = |type = Circular tumulus |area = Diameter: 50 meters |Construction = 5th century |burial = Clay coffin |Occupant = |burial goods = Steatite beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords |Historical designation1 = |notes = Destroyed }} '''Katonbo-yama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}) was a mid-[[Kofun period]] {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (tumulus) that once stood in {{ill|Mozu Akahata-cho|ja|百舌鳥赤畑町}}, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|excavations}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳}}, a reference mound Historic Site1 as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}. In 1949 (Showa 24), the mound was destroyed by soil extraction work carried out by contractors. The tumulus is estimated to have been a circular mound with a diameter of 50 meters. The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The burial goods unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual burial goods, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - A11roximately 20,000 beads. These steatite burial goods included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other burial goods included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, 11. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, 11. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} * {{ill|Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite burial goods and beads. == External Links == * {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun}} |name = Katonbo-yama Kofun |image = | part_of = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} |location = Mozu Akahata-cho, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = |type = Circular tumulus |area = Diameter: 50 meters |Construction = 5th century |burial = Clay coffin |Occupant = |burial goods = Steatite beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords |Historical designation1 = |notes = Destroyed }} '''Katonbo-yama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}) was a mid-[[Kofun period]] {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (tumulus) that once stood in {{ill|Mozu Akahata-cho|ja|百舌鳥赤畑町}}, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|excavations}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳}}, a reference mound Historic Site1 as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}. In 1949 (Showa 24), the mound was destroyed by soil extraction work carried out by contractors. The tumulus is estimated to have been a circular mound with a diameter of 50 meters. The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The burial goods unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual burial goods, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - A11roximately 20,000 beads. These steatite burial goods included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other burial goods included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, 11. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, 11. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} * {{ill|Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite burial goods and beads. == External Links == * It was part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|excavations}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳}}, a reference mound Historic Site1 as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}. In 1949 (Showa 24), the mound was destroyed by soil extraction work carried out by contractors. The tumulus is estimated to have been a circular mound with a diameter of 50 meters. The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The burial goods unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual burial goods, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - A11roximately 20,000 beads. These steatite burial goods included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other burial goods included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, 11. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, 11. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}} * {{ill|Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite burial goods and beads. == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html Katonbo-yama Kofun - Sakai City's Digital Kofun Encyclopedia] {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group|Katonboyama Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Katomboyama Kofun]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:aa]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[sn:Katomboyama Kofun]] [[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] [[en:Katomboyama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katonboyama Kofun}}

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |name = Katonbo-yama Kofun |image = | part_of = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}} |location = Mozu Akahata-cho, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = |type = Circular tumulus |area = Diameter: 50 meters |Construction = 5th century |burial = Clay coffin |Occupant = |burial goods = Steatite beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords |Historical designation1 = |notes = Destroyed }} '''Katonbo-yama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}) was a mid-[[Kofun period]] {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} (tumulus) that once stood in {{ill|Mozu Akahata-cho|ja|百舌鳥赤畑町|12=draft}}, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}}. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|excavations}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳|12=draft}}, a reference mound Historic Site1 as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft}}. In 1949 (Showa 24), the mound was destroyed by soil extraction work carried out by contractors. The tumulus is estimated to have been a circular mound with a diameter of 50 meters. The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=draft}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The burial goods unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual burial goods, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - A11roximately 20,000 beads. These steatite burial goods included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other burial goods included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡|12=draft}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, 11. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, 11. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳|12=draft}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite burial goods and beads. == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html Katonbo-yama Kofun - Sakai City's Digital Kofun Encyclopedia] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group|Katonboyama Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Katomboyama Kofun]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:aa]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[sn:Katomboyama Kofun]] [[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] [[en:Katomboyama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katonboyama Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}}

Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |name = Katonbo-yama Kofun |image = | part_of = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}} |location = Mozu Akahata-cho, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = |type = Circular tumulus |area = Diameter: 50 meters |Construction = 5th century |burial = Clay coffin |Occupant = |burial goods = Steatite beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords |Historical designation1 = |notes = Destroyed }} '''Katonbo-yama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}) was a mid-[[Kofun period]] {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} (tumulus) that once stood in {{ill|Mozu Akahata-cho|ja|百舌鳥赤畑町|12=draft}}, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}}. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|excavations}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳|12=draft}}, a reference mound Historic Site1 as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft}}. In 1949 (Showa 24), the mound was destroyed by soil extraction work carried out by contractors. The tumulus is estimated to have been a circular mound with a diameter of 50 meters. The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=draft}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The burial goods unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual burial goods, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - A11roximately 20,000 beads. These steatite burial goods included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other burial goods included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡|12=draft}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, 11. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, 11. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳|12=draft}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite burial goods and beads. == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html Katonbo-yama Kofun - Sakai City's Digital Kofun Encyclopedia] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group|Katonboyama Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Katomboyama Kofun]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:aa]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[sn:Katomboyama Kofun]] [[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] [[en:Katomboyama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katonboyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Katomboyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Katomboyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:qq]]

Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual burial goods, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - A11roximately 20,000 beads. These steatite burial goods included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other burial goods included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡|12=draft}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, 11. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, 11. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳|12=draft}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite burial goods and beads. == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html Katonbo-yama Kofun - Sakai City's Digital Kofun Encyclopedia] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group|Katonboyama Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Katomboyama Kofun]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:aa]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[sn:Katomboyama Kofun]] [[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] [[en:Katomboyama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katonboyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Katomboyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Katomboyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |name = Katonbo-yama Kofun |image = | part_of = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}} |location = Mozu Akahata-cho, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = |type = Circular tumulus |area = Diameter: 50 meters |Construction = 5th century |burial = Clay coffin |Occupant = |burial goods = Steatite beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords |Historical designation1 = |notes = Destroyed }} '''Katonbo-yama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}) was a mid-[[Kofun period]] {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} (tumulus) that once stood in {{ill|Mozu Akahata-cho|ja|百舌鳥赤畑町|12=draft}}, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}}. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|excavations}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳|12=draft}}, a reference mound Historic Site1 as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft}}. In 1949 (Showa 24), the mound was destroyed by soil extraction work carried out by contractors. The tumulus is estimated to have been a circular mound with a diameter of 50 meters. The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=draft}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The burial goods unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual burial goods, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - A11roximately 20,000 beads. These steatite burial goods included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other burial goods included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡|12=draft}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, 11. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, 11. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳|12=draft}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite burial goods and beads. == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html Katonbo-yama Kofun - Sakai City's Digital Kofun Encyclopedia] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group|Katonboyama Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Katomboyama Kofun]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:aa]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[sn:Katomboyama Kofun]] [[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] [[en:Katomboyama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katonboyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Katomboyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Katomboyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]]

It was part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}}. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|excavations}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳|12=draft}}, a reference mound Historic Site1 as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft}}. In 1949 (Showa 24), the mound was destroyed by soil extraction work carried out by contractors. The tumulus is estimated to have been a circular mound with a diameter of 50 meters. The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=draft}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The burial goods unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual burial goods, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - A11roximately 20,000 beads. These steatite burial goods included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other burial goods included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡|12=draft}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, 11. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, 11. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳|12=draft}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite burial goods and beads. == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html Katonbo-yama Kofun - Sakai City's Digital Kofun Encyclopedia] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group|Katonboyama Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Katomboyama Kofun]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:aa]] }} [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[sn:Katomboyama Kofun]] [[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] [[en:Katomboyama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katonboyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Katomboyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Katomboyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |name = Katonbo-yama Kofun |image = | part_of = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}} |location = Mozu Akahata-cho, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = |type = Circular tumulus |area = Diameter: 50 meters |Construction = 5th century |burial = Clay coffin |Occupant = |burial goods = Steatite beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords |Historical designation1 = |notes = Destroyed }} '''Katonbo-yama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}) was a mid-[[Kofun period]] {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} (tumulus) that once stood in {{ill|Mozu Akahata-cho|ja|百舌鳥赤畑町|12=draft}}, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}}. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|excavations}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳|12=draft}}, a reference mound Historic Site1 as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft}}. In 1949 (Showa 24), the mound was destroyed by soil extraction work carried out by contractors. The tumulus is estimated to have been a circular mound with a diameter of 50 meters. The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=draft}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The burial goods unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual burial goods, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - A11roximately 20,000 beads. These steatite burial goods included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other burial goods included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡|12=draft}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, 11. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, 11. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳|12=draft}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite burial goods and beads. == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html Katonbo-yama Kofun - Sakai City's Digital Kofun Encyclopedia] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group|Katonboyama Kofun]] [[Category:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Katomboyama Kofun]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[sn:Katomboyama Kofun]] [[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] [[en:Katomboyama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katonboyama Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Katomboyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Katomboyama Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=draft}} |name = Katonbo-yama Kofun |image = | part_of = {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}} |location = Mozu Akahata-cho, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan |coordinates = |type = Circular tumulus |area = Diameter: 50 meters |built = 5th century |burial = Clay coffin |Occupant = |burial goods = Steatite beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords |Historical designation1 = |notes = Destroyed }} '''Katonbo-yama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}) was a mid-[[Kofun period]] {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} (tumulus) that once stood in {{ill|Mozu Akahata-cho|ja|百舌鳥赤畑町|12=draft}}, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}}. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|excavations}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳|12=draft}}, a reference mound Historic Site1 as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site|12=draft}}. In 1949 (Showa 24), the mound was destroyed by soil extraction work carried out by contractors. The tumulus is estimated to have been a circular mound with a diameter of 50 meters. The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=draft}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The burial goods unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual burial goods, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - A11roximately 20,000 beads. These steatite burial goods included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other burial goods included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡|12=draft}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, 11. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, 11. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs|12=draft}} * {{ill|Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳|12=draft}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite burial goods and beads. == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html Katonbo-yama Kofun - Sakai City's Digital Kofun Encyclopedia] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group|Katonboyama Kofun]] [[Category:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Katomboyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] }} [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[sn:Katomboyama Kofun]] [[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] [[en:Katomboyama Kofun]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Katonboyama Kofun}}

It was part of the {{ill|Mozu Kofun Group|ja|百舌鳥古墳群|1=Mozu Tombs}}. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|excavations}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳}}, a reference mound Historic Site1 as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Mausoleum Reference Site}}. The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The burial goods unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual burial goods, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - A11roximately 20,000 beads. These steatite burial goods included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other burial goods included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama

revision.comment: 24 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:カトンボ山古墳 up to rev 101653195
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = カトンボ山古墳 |画像 = |所属=[[百舌鳥古墳群]] |所在地 = 大阪府堺市北区百舌鳥赤畑町 |位置 = |形状 = 円墳 |規模 = 径50ｍ　 |築造年代 = 5世紀 |埋葬施設 = 粘土槨 |被葬者 = |出土品 = 滑石製玉類　銅鏡　鉄刀 |史跡指定 = |特記事項 = 消滅 }} '''カトンボ山古墳'''（かとんぼやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]]北区赤畑町にかつてあった[[古墳時代]]中期の[[古墳]]（[[円墳]]）である。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]の中の1基であった。 == 概要== [[ファイル:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|子持勾玉（凹面側）}}{{small|[[東京国立博物館]]展示。}}]] [[ファイル:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|子持勾玉（凸面側）}}{{small|[[鳥取県立博物館]]企画展示時に撮影。}}]] [[ファイル:カトンボ山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|出土品}}{{small|東京国立博物館蔵。[[松戸市立博物館]]企画展示時に撮影。}}]] [[陵墓参考地]]の[[御廟山古墳]]の後円部の濠外にあり、昭和24年（1949年）に土取り業者による採土工事によって消滅している。直径50メートルの円墳と推定されている。土取りによる破壊が突如行われたが、緊急調査が学生考古学研究会（現在の古代学研究会）の[[森浩一]]らによって行われている。出土した遺物は通常、古墳副葬品とよんでいるものとはかなり異なり、[[滑石]]製の祭祀遺物を大量に含んでいた。滑石製品は、子持勾玉4個、[[勾玉]]725個、斧頭6個、鎌13個、刀子160個、双孔円板1個、鍬形（剣身形）1個、臼玉約2万個があった。このうち斧頭、鎌、刀子は本来は鉄製利器であるものを[[祭祀]]用として滑石で製作しており、実用には使えない石製模造品である。また、勾玉も装身具ではなく滑石で簡便に製作したものであり、臼玉もガラス製小玉などの代用品であろうとしている。玉類に孔が貫通しているのは自然として、斧頭、鎌、刀子にも必ず1孔か2孔あけられており、これらの使用方法としては、玉類を延々と連ねて、おそらく樹木の枝にかけ、斧頭、鎌、刀子もそれぞれ個々に懸垂させ、クリスマスツリーのように飾る[[神事]]に使われたものではないかとしている。滑石製品のほかには2面の[[銅鏡]]（位至三公鏡と無文鏡）と刀、剣、矛、鏃、刀子、斧頭、蜘蛛手形鉄器などの鉄製品があったが、百舌鳥古墳群や[[古市古墳群]]の古墳によく見られる甲冑類がなく、滑石製品が大量に埋納され、[[朱]]を使用した形跡もなく、しかも人体埋葬の行なわれたことを示す資料もなかったようである。大型古墳に附属した位置にある陪塚に大量に遺物を埋納しながら、人体埋葬の形跡のない類例の1つではないかとされている。 カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|子持勾玉
{{small|東京国立博物館展示。}} == 参考文献 == * [[森浩一]]『大阪府史　第一巻』大阪府史編纂委員会 1978年 692-694頁 * 森浩一　「カトンボ山古墳の発掘」『僕は考古学に鍛えられた』ちくま文庫　も26-1　筑摩書房　2012年　182-184頁 == 関連項目== * [[百舌鳥古墳群]] * [[野中古墳]] - カトンボ山古墳と同様に多量の滑石製模造品・臼玉が埋納された陪塚。 == 外部リンク== * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html カトンボ山古墳 市デジタル古墳百科] {{デフォルトソート:かとんほやまこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:円墳]] [[Category:百舌鳥古墳群|消かとんほやまこふん]]

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = カトンボ山古墳 |画像 = |所属=[[百舌鳥古墳群]] |所在地 = 大阪府堺市北区百舌鳥赤畑町 |位置 = |形状 = 円墳 |規模 = 径50ｍ　 |築造年代 = 5世紀 |埋葬施設 = 粘土槨 |被葬者 = |出土品 = 滑石製玉類　銅鏡　鉄刀 |史跡指定 = |特記事項 = 消滅 }} '''カトンボ山古墳'''（かとんぼやまこふん）は、[[大阪府]][[堺市]]北区赤畑町にかつてあった[[古墳時代]]中期の[[古墳]]（[[円墳]]）である。[[百舌鳥古墳群]]の中の1基であった。 == 概要== [[ファイル:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|子持勾玉（凹面側）}}{{small|[[東京国立博物館]]展示。}}]] [[ファイル:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|子持勾玉（凸面側）}}{{small|[[鳥取県立博物館]]企画展示時に撮影。}}]] [[ファイル:カトンボ山古墳 出土品.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|出土品}}{{small|東京国立博物館蔵。[[松戸市立博物館]]企画展示時に撮影。}}]] [[陵墓参考地]]の[[御廟山古墳]]の後円部の濠外にあり、昭和24年（1949年）に土取り業者による採土工事によって消滅している。直径50メートルの円墳と推定されている。土取りによる破壊が突如行われたが、緊急調査が学生考古学研究会（現在の古代学研究会）の[[森浩一]]らによって行われている。出土した遺物は通常、古墳副葬品とよんでいるものとはかなり異なり、[[滑石]]製の祭祀遺物を大量に含んでいた。滑石製品は、子持勾玉4個、[[勾玉]]725個、斧頭6個、鎌13個、刀子160個、双孔円板1個、鍬形（剣身形）1個、臼玉約2万個があった。このうち斧頭、鎌、刀子は本来は鉄製利器であるものを[[祭祀]]用として滑石で製作しており、実用には使えない石製模造品である。また、勾玉も装身具ではなく滑石で簡便に製作したものであり、臼玉もガラス製小玉などの代用品であろうとしている。玉類に孔が貫通しているのは自然として、斧頭、鎌、刀子にも必ず1孔か2孔あけられており、これらの使用方法としては、玉類を延々と連ねて、おそらく樹木の枝にかけ、斧頭、鎌、刀子もそれぞれ個々に懸垂させ、クリスマスツリーのように飾る[[神事]]に使われたものではないかとしている。滑石製品のほかには2面の[[銅鏡]]（位至三公鏡と無文鏡）と刀、剣、矛、鏃、刀子、斧頭、蜘蛛手形鉄器などの鉄製品があったが、百舌鳥古墳群や[[古市古墳群]]の古墳によく見られる甲冑類がなく、滑石製品が大量に埋納され、[[朱]]を使用した形跡もなく、しかも人体埋葬の行なわれたことを示す資料もなかったようである。大型古墳に附属した位置にある陪塚に大量に遺物を埋納しながら、人体埋葬の形跡のない類例の1つではないかとされている。 カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|子持勾玉
{{small|東京国立博物館展示。}} == 参考文献 == * [[森浩一]]『大阪府史　第一巻』大阪府史編纂委員会 1978年 692-694頁 * 森浩一　「カトンボ山古墳の発掘」『僕は考古学に鍛えられた』ちくま文庫　も26-1　筑摩書房　2012年　182-184頁 == 関連項目== * [[百舌鳥古墳群]] * [[野中古墳]] - カトンボ山古墳と同様に多量の滑石製模造品・臼玉が埋納された陪塚。 == 外部リンク== * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html カトンボ山古墳 市デジタル古墳百科] {{デフォルトソート:かとんほやまこふん}} [[category:大阪府の古墳]] [[category:円墳]] [[Category:百舌鳥古墳群|消かとんほやまこふん]]
