{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, 11. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, 11. 182–184. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] * {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|lt=Nonaka Kofun|lt=Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nonomura Kofun|qq=}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite burial goods and beads. == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html Katonbo-yama Kofun - Sakai City's Digital Kofun Encyclopedia] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|カトンボ山古墳|version=101653195|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group|Katonboyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] {{Commons category|Katonboyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q17219645}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

[[commons:Category:Katonboyama Kofun]][[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Katonboyama Kofun]][[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17219645]] Wikidata name: Katonboyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Katonbo-yama Kofun |image = | part_of = {{ill|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584}} |location = {{ill|Kita-ku|WD=Q1042504}} |coordinates = |type = Circular tumulus |area = Diameter: 50 meters |built = 5th century |burial = Clay coffin |Occupant = |burial goods = Steatite beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords |Historical designation1 = |notes = Destroyed |architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} '''Katonbo-yama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}) was a mid-[[Kofun period]] [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (tumulus) that once stood in {{ill|Mozu Akahata-cho|ja|百舌鳥赤畑町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu Akahata-cho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It was part of the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]]. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|excavations}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}, a reference mound Historic Site1 as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Tomb Reference Site|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}. In 1949 (Showa 24), the mound was destroyed by soil extraction work carried out by contractors. The tumulus is estimated to have been a circular mound with a diameter of 50 meters. The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koichi Mori|1=Kōichi Mori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539977}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The burial goods unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual burial goods, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - Approximately20,000 beads. These steatite burial goods included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other burial goods included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/位至三公鏡|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, 11. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, 11. 182–184. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] * {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|lt=Nonaka Kofun|lt=Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nonomura Kofun|qq=}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite burial goods and beads. == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html Katonbo-yama Kofun - Sakai City's Digital Kofun Encyclopedia] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|カトンボ山古墳|version=101653195|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group|Katonboyama Kofun]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] {{Commons category|Katonboyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q17219645}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

It was part of the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]]. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|excavations}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}, a reference mound Historic Site1 as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Tomb Reference Site|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}. In 1949 (Showa 24), the mound was destroyed by soil extraction work carried out by contractors. The tumulus is estimated to have been a circular mound with a diameter of 50 meters. The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koichi Mori|1=Kōichi Mori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539977}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The burial goods unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual burial goods, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - Approximately20,000 beads. These steatite burial goods included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other burial goods included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/位至三公鏡|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, 11. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, 11. 182–184. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] * {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|lt=Nonaka Kofun|lt=Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nonomura Kofun|qq=}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite burial goods and beads. == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html Katonbo-yama Kofun - Sakai City's Digital Kofun Encyclopedia] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|カトンボ山古墳|version=101653195|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] {{Commons category|Katonboyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q17219645}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group|Katonboyama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, 11. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, 11. 182–184. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] * {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|lt=Nonaka Kofun|lt=Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nonomura Kofun|qq=}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite burial goods and beads. == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html Katonbo-yama Kofun - Sakai City's Digital Kofun Encyclopedia] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|カトンボ山古墳|version=101653195|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] {{Commons category|Katonboyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q17219645}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group|Katonboyama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Katonboyama Kofun]][[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Katonboyama Kofun]][[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Katonboyama Kofun]][[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Katonboyama Kofun]][[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q17219645]] Wikidata name: Katonboyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:26, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Katonbo-yama Kofun |image = | part_of = {{ill|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584}} |location = {{ill|Kita-ku|WD=Q1042504}} |coordinates = |type = Circular tumulus |area = Diameter: 50 meters |built = 5th century |burial = Clay coffin |Occupant = |burial goods = Steatite beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords |Historical designation1 = |notes = Destroyed |architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} '''Katonbo-yama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}) was a mid-[[Kofun period]] [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (tumulus) that once stood in {{ill|Mozu Akahata-cho|ja|百舌鳥赤畑町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu Akahata-cho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It was part of the [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]]. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|excavations}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}, a reference mound Historic Site1 as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Tomb Reference Site|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}. In 1949 (Showa 24), the mound was destroyed by soil extraction work carried out by contractors. The tumulus is estimated to have been a circular mound with a diameter of 50 meters. The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koichi Mori|1=Kōichi Mori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539977}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The burial goods unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual burial goods, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - Approximately20,000 beads. These steatite burial goods included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other burial goods included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/位至三公鏡|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, 11. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, 11. 182–184. == Related Articles == * [[:en:Mozu Tombs|Mozu Tombs]] * {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|lt=Nonaka Kofun|lt=Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nonomura Kofun|qq=}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite burial goods and beads. == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html Katonbo-yama Kofun - Sakai City's Digital Kofun Encyclopedia] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|カトンボ山古墳|version=101653195|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] {{Commons category|Katonboyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q17219645}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group|Katonboyama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

It was part of the {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs}}. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|excavations}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}, a reference mound Historic Site1 as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Tomb Reference Site|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}. {{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, 11. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, 11. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs}} * {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|lt=Nonaka Kofun|lt=Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nonomura Kofun|qq=}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite burial goods and beads. == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html Katonbo-yama Kofun - Sakai City's Digital Kofun Encyclopedia] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|カトンボ山古墳|version=101653195|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] {{Commons category|Katonboyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q17219645}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group|Katonboyama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Katonboyama Kofun]][[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Katonboyama Kofun]][[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Katonboyama Kofun]][[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] [[sn:commons:Category:Katonboyama Kofun]][[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Katonbo-yama Kofun |image = | part_of = {{ill|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584}} |location = {{ill|Kita-ku|WD=Q1042504}} |coordinates = |type = Circular tumulus |area = Diameter: 50 meters |built = 5th century |burial = Clay coffin |Occupant = |burial goods = Steatite beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords |Historical designation1 = |notes = Destroyed |architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747}} }} '''Katonbo-yama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}) was a mid-[[Kofun period]] {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} (tumulus) that once stood in {{ill|Mozu Akahata-cho|ja|百舌鳥赤畑町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu Akahata-cho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs}}. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|excavations}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}, a reference mound Historic Site1 as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Tomb Reference Site|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}. In 1949 (Showa 24), the mound was destroyed by soil extraction work carried out by contractors. The tumulus is estimated to have been a circular mound with a diameter of 50 meters. The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koichi Mori|1=Kōichi Mori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539977}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The burial goods unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual burial goods, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - Approximately20,000 beads. These steatite burial goods included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other burial goods included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/位至三公鏡|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, 11. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, 11. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs}} * {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|lt=Nonaka Kofun|lt=Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nonomura Kofun|qq=}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite burial goods and beads. == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html Katonbo-yama Kofun - Sakai City's Digital Kofun Encyclopedia] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|カトンボ山古墳|version=101653195|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] {{Commons category|Katonboyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q17219645}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group|Katonboyama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

[[co:commons:Category:Katonboyama Kofun]][[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Katonboyama Kofun]][[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Katonboyama Kofun]][[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] [[Category:sn_crud commons:Category:Katonboyama Kofun]][[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Katonbo-yama Kofun |image = | part_of = {{ill|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|1=Mozu Tombs}} |location = {{ill|Kita-ku|WD=Q1042504|1=Kita-ku, Sakai}} |coordinates = |type = Circular tumulus |area = Diameter: 50 meters |built = 5th century |burial = Clay coffin |Occupant = |burial goods = Steatite beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords |Historical designation1 = |notes = Destroyed |architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} }} '''Katonbo-yama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}) was a mid-[[Kofun period]] {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} (tumulus) that once stood in {{ill|Mozu Akahata-cho|ja|百舌鳥赤畑町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu Akahata-cho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs|1=Mozu Tombs}}. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|excavations}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}, a reference mound Historic Site1 as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Tomb Reference Site|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}. In 1949 (Showa 24), the mound was destroyed by soil extraction work carried out by contractors. The tumulus is estimated to have been a circular mound with a diameter of 50 meters. The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koichi Mori|1=Kōichi Mori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539977}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The burial goods unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual burial goods, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - Approximately20,000 beads. These steatite burial goods included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other burial goods included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/位至三公鏡|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, 11. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, 11. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs|1=Mozu Tombs}} * {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|lt=Nonaka Kofun|lt=Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nonomura Kofun|qq=}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite burial goods and beads. == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html Katonbo-yama Kofun - Sakai City's Digital Kofun Encyclopedia] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|カトンボ山古墳|version=101653195|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] {{Commons category|Katonboyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q17219645}} [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group|Katonboyama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

[[co:commons:Category:Katonboyama Kofun]][[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Katonboyama Kofun]][[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] [[co:commons:Category:Katonboyama Kofun]][[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Katonboyama Kofun]][[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |name = Katonbo-yama Kofun |image = | part_of = {{ill|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|1=Mozu Tombs}} |location = {{ill|Kita-ku|WD=Q1042504|1=Kita-ku, Sakai}} |coordinates = |type = Circular tumulus |area = Diameter: 50 meters |built = 5th century |burial = Clay coffin |Occupant = |burial goods = Steatite beads, bronze mirrors, iron swords |Historical designation1 = |notes = Destroyed |architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|WD=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} }} '''Katonbo-yama Kofun''' ({{lang|ja|カトンボ山古墳}}) was a mid-[[Kofun period]] {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} (tumulus) that once stood in {{ill|Mozu Akahata-cho|ja|百舌鳥赤畑町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mozu Akahata-cho|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kita-ku, Sakai City, Osaka Prefecture, Japan. It was part of the {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs|1=Mozu Tombs}}. == Overview == [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (concave side)}}{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳出土 子持勾玉-2.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Komochi magatama (convex side)}}{{small|Photographed during an exhibition at the Tottori Prefectural Museum.}}]] [[File:カトンボ山古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|thumb|220px|right|{{center|excavations}}{{small|In the collection of the Tokyo National Museum, photographed during an exhibition at the Matsudo City Museum.}}]] The tumulus was located just outside the outer moat of the {{ill|Gobyoyama Kofun|ja|御廟山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}}, a reference mound Historic Site1 as an {{ill|Imperial Tomb Reference Site|ja|陵墓参考地|1=Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imperial Tomb Reference Site|13=User:Immanuelle/Candidate Imperial Mausoleum|qq=}}. In 1949 (Showa 24), the mound was destroyed by soil extraction work carried out by contractors. The tumulus is estimated to have been a circular mound with a diameter of 50 meters. The sudden destruction prompted an emergency excavation led by archaeologist {{ill|Koichi Mori|ja|森浩一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koichi Mori|1=Kōichi Mori|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11539977}} and members of a student archaeological research society (now the Society of Ancient Studies). The burial goods unearthed were markedly different from typical burial goods associated with ancient tumuli. Among the finds were numerous steatite ritual burial goods, including: - 4 komochi magatama (parent-and-child magatama), - 725 magatama, - 6 axe heads, - 13 sickles, - 160 knives, - 1 double-hole disc, - 1 spade-shaped object, - Approximately20,000 beads. These steatite burial goods included axe heads, sickles, and knives, which were modeled on iron tools but crafted for ritual purposes. The magatama and other items were simplified versions made from steatite, suggesting they were substitutes for more typical ornaments or glass beads. The tools and ornaments were likely strung together and suspended—possibly from tree branches—resembling a decorated ritual structure akin to a Christmas tree. Other burial goods included two bronze mirrors (a {{ill|位至三公鏡|ja|位至三公鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/位至三公鏡|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and a plain mirror), as well as iron weapons such as swords, spears, arrowheads, and unique multi-pronged implements. However, no armor, vermillion traces, or evidence of human burial was discovered, distinguishing the mound from other contemporaneous tumuli. It is hypothesized that this mound may have been an accessory mound of a larger kofun, designed to house ritual deposits rather than human remains. カトンボ山古墳 子持勾玉 (J-36628-4).JPG|Komochi magatama
{{small|On display at the Tokyo National Museum.}} == References == * Koichi Mori, ''Osaka Prefectural History, Volume I,'' Osaka Prefectural History Editorial Committee, 1978, 11. 692–694. * Koichi Mori, "Excavation of Katonbo-yama Kofun," in ''I Was Trained by Archaeology,'' Chikuma Bunko, Vol. 26-1, Chikuma Shobo, 2012, 11. 182–184. == Related Articles == * {{ill|Mozu Tombs|en|Mozu Tombs|WD=Q4250584|lt=Mozu Tombs|1=Mozu Tombs}} * {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|lt=Nonaka Kofun|lt=Nonomura Kofun|ja|野中古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nonomura Kofun|qq=}} - Another accompanying tumulus with similar steatite burial goods and beads. == External Links == * [http://www.city.sakai.lg.jp/kofun/database/katonboyama.html Katonbo-yama Kofun - Sakai City's Digital Kofun Encyclopedia] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|カトンボ山古墳|version=101653195|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[fr:Katombo-yama Kofun]] [[ja:カトンボ山古墳]] {{Commons category|Katonboyama Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q17219645}} [[Category:Kofun of Osaka prefecture]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group]] [[Category:Mozu Kofun Group|Katonboyama Kofun]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
